videoDescription:

By the end, you will have a deep understanding of the concepts and a great overall big picture of how Docker is used in the whole software development process.

videoTitle: Expert Talk: DevOps & Software Architecture • Simon Brown, Dave Farley & Hannes Lowette • GOTO 2021
videoDescription:
This interview was recorded at GOTO Copenhagen for GOTO Unscripted. #GOTOcon #GOTOunscripted #GOTOcph
https://gotopia.tech

Read the full transcription of this interview here:
https://gotopia.tech/articles/expert-talk-devops-software-architecture

Simon Brown - Author of "Software Architecture for Developers" & Creator of the C4 Software Architecture Model @simonbrown4821 
Dave Farley - Author of "Modern Software Engineering" Continuous Delivery & DevOps Pioneer @ModernSoftwareEngineeringYT 
Hannes Lowette - Head of Learning & Development at Axxes @Belenar82 

RESOURCES
https://youtu.be/D43PlUr1x_E • Alan Kay • GOTO 2021
https://youtu.be/lLnsi522LS8 • Mary Shaw • GOTO 2015
https://youtu.be/5OjqD-ow8GE • Simon Brown • GOTO 2018

DESCRIPTION
As software architecture continues to evolve rapidly, we are constantly confronted with new challenges. Simon Brown, Dave Farley and Hannes Lowette cover some of the recent trends in software architecture touching on terms such as DevOps and how to deal with complexity. They also reference concepts that have stirred debates forever and are still not done right, like bounded context and continuous delivery.

TIMECODES
00:00 Intro
00:53 Evolution in software architecture
06:16 Managing complexity
14:22 Bounded context
22:00 Problems in our industry
25:00 Continuous delivery done right
28:56 DevOps: just a title?
31:47 Similarities between software architecture & architecture
39:23 Outro

RECOMMENDED BOOKS
Simon Brown • Software Architecture for Developers Vol. 2 • https://leanpub.com/visualising-software-architecture
David Farley • Modern Software Engineering • https://amzn.to/3GI468M
Dave Farley & Jez Humble • Continuous Delivery • https://amzn.to/3ocIHwd

videoTitle: Docker Tutorial for Beginners - A Full DevOps Course on How to Run Applications in Containers
videoDescription:
Get started using Docker with this end-to-end beginners course with hands-on labs.

Docker is an open platform for developers and sysadmins to build, ship, and run distributed applications, whether on laptops, data center VMs, or the cloud.

In this course you will learn Docker through a series of lectures that use animation, illustration and some fun analogies that simply complex concepts, we have demos that will show how to install and get started with Docker and most importantly we have hands-on labs that you can access right in your browser.

videoTitle: What is DevOps? - In Simple English
videoDescription:
Simple explanation of DevOps without the complications and information overload.  -- what do you think? Please share & comment! 

DevOps is a concept with different interpretations and definitions, but when you get down to it, it's all about developers and operations teams breaking down silos and working together to innovate faster. For many companies, the ability to innovate at a rapid pace — responding to market conditions and customer feedback — is a key factor for success. Adopting the DevOps philosophy requires a new mindset, new tools and new skills.

More about DevOps:

videoTitle: DevOps Engineering Course for Beginners
videoDescription:
What is DevOps in the context of software engineering? Learn all about DevOps in this comprehensive course for beginners with three technical tutorials. You will learn what DevOps is, continuous integration, continuous deployment strategies, and application performance management. Many DevOps practices are commonly used in programming and web development, it is important to understand key terms and technologies. 

This course often references the MERN (MongoDB, Express JS, React JS, Node JS) technology stack. You will get a series of talks with technology recommendations based on these best practices. There will be several programming examples embedded in these talks. As long as you know the absolute basics of coding and the web, you'll have no problem following along. 

⭐️ Course Notes ⭐️
🔗 Lesson 1: https://layerci.com/blog/what-is-devops/

videoTitle: ChatGPT Tutorial - Use ChatGPT for DevOps tasks to 10x Your Productivity
videoDescription:
ChatGPT Tutorial for DevOps - What is ChatGPT? How to use ChatGPT? My opinion on whether ChatGPT will replace engineers
💛  Follow me on LinkedIn and IG for weekly updates   ►  https://bit.ly/2F3LXYJ
💙  Become a DevOps Engineer - 6-month program       ►  https://bit.ly/43nK7ra
💚  Mini-Bootcamp for IT Beginners                                  ►  https://bit.ly/3BRz6To

#chatgpt   #techworldwithnana 

►  This video is sponsored by Firefly 🙌🏼
►  For more infos: https://eu1.hubs.ly/H02z-Lf0
►  Open source tool AIaC: https://eu1.hubs.ly/H02z-GD0

I'm sure you have all heard of ChatGPT by now. It has become a buzzword within days of its release and professionals in all fields, especially in high skilled areas like lawyers, doctors, engineers are questioning whether such AI can actually replace them and work.

So in this video I want to talk about what ChatGPT is and how it even popped up, talk a bit about the organization behind GPT called "OpenAI", which has already created many other machine learning models besides Chat GPT and also explain technically about all that.
And then we'll dive in and actually put ChatGPT to use for some DevOps related tasks.
I really want to see how it can help in generating configuration code for building DevOps processes or different parts of those processes and how well it knows different DevOps technologies, but not just some shallow examples or boilerplate code that I can get from official documentation, but instead also try more fine-tuning and small optimizations in that configuration code.

We're also going to check out an open source command line tool that is built on top of ChatGPT and was specifically created for engineers to generate infrastructure as code templates and more and finally we'll talk about the impact of ChatGPT, the quality and usefulness of such a tool for engineers and whether it will really replace the engineers and to what extent you should be concerned.

53:53 - Tools built on top of OpenAI's API
55:01 - AIaC demo - CLI tool for DevOps
01:01:00 - My opinion on ChatGPT & whether ChatGPT will replace engineers

#edureka #DevOpsEdureka #DevOps #DevOpsTools #DevOpsTraining #DevOpsTutorial #DevOpsJenkins

About the Course
Edureka’s DevOps online training is designed to help you master key tools of Devops lifecycle like Docker, Puppet, Jenkins, Nagios, GIT, Ansible, SaltStack and Chef used by a DevOps Engineer for automating multiple steps in SDLC. During this course, our expert DevOps instructors will help you:

1. Understand the concepts and necessities of DevOps
2. Understand the need for DevOps and the day-to-day real-life problems it resolves
3. Learn installation and configuration of common infrastructure servers like Apache, and Nginx for the Enterprise
4. Learn popular DevOps tools like Jenkins, Puppet, Chef, Ansible, SaltStack, Nagios and GIT
5. Implement automated system update, installations and deployments 
6. Learn Virtualization Concepts 
7. Configuration deployment and packaging, continuous integration using GIT
8. Fine tune Performance and set-up basic Security for Infrastructure 
9. Manage server operations using Code which is popularly known as Infrastructure as a Code
10. Understand the need for and concepts of Monitoring and Logging. 

Along with the above mentioned topics, to help you master the most popular DevOps tools, you will also receive 3 additional self-paced courses including presentations, class recordings, assignments, solutions for the following tools:

1: Ansible - Covers Introduction, Setup & Configuration, Ansible Playbooks, 37 Ansible Modules, Different Roles and Command Line usage. 2: Chef - Covers Introduction, Building the Cook Book, Node Object & Search, Data-bags, Chef environment, Roles, Deploying Nodes in Production and using the Open Source Chef Server.

3: Puppet - Covers Puppet Infrastructure & run-cycle, the Puppet Language, Environment defining Nodes and Modules, Provisioning a Web Server and Executing Modules Against A Puppet Master.

Who should go for this course?
DevOps practitioners are among the highest paid IT professionals today, and the market demand for them is growing rapidly. With emergence of new job roles around DevOps philosophy, anyone aspiring to get into these new roles, can take up this DevOps course. Some of these roles are:

1. DevOps Architect
2. Automation Engineer
3. Software Tester
4. Security Engineer
5. Integration Specialist
6. Release Manager

Project Work
1. Host a dummy webpage using Apache Web Server.
2. Write shell script which reports:
a) Various system configurations related to the user and the OS.
b) Data related to load on the server.
c) Top 5 processes with maximum number of threads.
d) Sort the services by memory
3. Install Nagios on a VM node for monitoring the various parameter of the VM.

#Edureka #EdurekaDevOps #DevOpsTutorial #DevOpsTools #DevOpsTraining #Git #Jenkins #Docker #Puppet #Nagios #DevOpsUseCase

- - - - - - - - - - - - - - 

About the Course
Edureka’s DevOps online training is designed to help you master key tools of Devops lifecycle like Docker, Puppet, Jenkins, Nagios, GIT, Ansible, SaltStack and Chef used by a DevOps Engineer for automating multiple steps in SDLC. During this course, our expert DevOps instructors will help you:

1. Understand the concepts and necessities of DevOps
2. Understand the need for DevOps and the day-to-day real-life problems it resolves
3. Learn installation and configuration of common infrastructure servers like Apache, and Nginx for the Enterprise
4. Learn popular DevOps tools like Jenkins, Puppet, Chef, Ansible, SaltStack, Nagios and GIT
5. Implement automated system update, installations and deployments 
6. Learn Virtualization Concepts 
7. Configuration deployment and packaging, continuous integration using GIT
8. Fine tune Performance and set-up basic Security for Infrastructure 
9. Manage server operations using Code which is popularly known as Infrastructure as a Code
10. Understand the need for and concepts of Monitoring and Logging. 

Along with the above mentioned topics, to help you master the most popular DevOps tools, you will also receive 3 additional self-paced courses including presentations, class recordings, assignments, solutions for the following tools:

1: Ansible - Covers Introduction, Setup & Configuration, Ansible Playbooks, 37 Ansible Modules, Different Roles and Command Line usage. 2: Chef - Covers Introduction, Building the Cook Book, Node Object & Search, Data-bags, Chef environment, Roles, Deploying Nodes in Production and using the Open Source Chef Server.

3: Puppet - Covers Puppet Infrastructure & run-cycle, the Puppet Language, Environment defining Nodes and Modules, Provisioning a Web Server and Executing Modules Against A Puppet Master.

- - - - - - - - - - - - - - 

Who should go for this course?
DevOps practitioners are among the highest paid IT professionals today, and the market demand for them is growing rapidly. With emergence of new job roles around DevOps philosophy, anyone aspiring to get into these new roles, can take up this DevOps course. Some of these roles are:

1. DevOps Architect
2. Automation Engineer
3. Software Tester
4. Security Engineer
5. Integration Specialist
6. Release Manager

- - - - - - - - - - - - - - 

Project Work
1. Host a dummy webpage using Apache Web Server.
2. Write shell script which reports:
a) Various system configurations related to the user and the OS.
b) Data related to load on the server.
c) Top 5 processes with maximum number of threads.
d) Sort the services by memory
3. Install Nagios on a VM node for monitoring the various parameter of the VM.

videoTitle: The ONLY Roadmap to Get a DevOps Job in 2026 | Zero to Hero Guidance | Senior DevOps Engineer | USA
videoDescription:
This is the exact roadmap that will get you hired in 2026. 🚀

Stop following random roadmaps that leave you more confused than when you started. The DevOps landscape is shifting, and simply knowing a few tools isn't enough anymore. To land a high-paying role today, you need to understand the logic, the architecture, and the "why" behind every deployment.

I’m Avi (Vamsi Avinash Gunji). I’ve spent years navigating the enterprise tech world—from my first role at Clarivate in 2019 to roles at Farmers Insurance, The Home Depot, PwC, and Goldman Sachs, leading up to my current position as a Senior DevOps Engineer. I’ve seen what actually works in production and what’s just "tutorial noise."

In this video, I’m breaking down the definitive path I would follow if I were starting from zero today. We’re debugging your career "Desi style"—from code errors to chai breaks. This is my narrative—unfiltered, evolving, and unfinished.

📍 The Roadmap Breakdown:
In this video, we cover the critical phases to master:
✅ Phase 1: Mastering the Linux terminal, systemctl, and Bash/Python automation.
✅ Phase 1.2: Why Networking (DNS/VPCs) and Storage (NFS) are your best friends.
✅ Phase 2: Cloud architecture (AWS/Azure/GCP) vs. the reality of Private Cloud and On-Prem.
✅ Phase 3: Infrastructure as Code (Terraform & Ansible) to save you from disasters.
✅ Phase 4: Containers & Kubernetes (K8s)—The core of modern engineering.
✅ Phase 4.2: Building actual end-to-end CI/CD pipelines with Jenkins and GitHub Actions.
✅ Phase 5: Monitoring, Alerting, and Observability (Prometheus, Grafana, Splunk).

🕒 TIMESTAMPS:

00:00 Intro
00:41 Phase 1: Linux Foundations
01:40 Phase 1.2: Networking and Storage
02:48 Phase 2: The Cloud Ecosystem
04:02 Phase 3: Infrastructure as Code
04:57 Phase 4: Containers & Kubernetes
06:18 Phase 4.2: CI/CD Pipelines
06:47 Phase 5: Monitoring & Alerting
07:28 Conclusion: Senior Tip


🔗 Official Resources & Certifications:
Linux Foundation (LFCS): https://training.linuxfoundation.org/certification/linux-foundation-certified-sysadmin-lfcs/ 

AWS DevOps Engineer Certification: https://aws.amazon.com/certification/certified-devops-engineer-professional/

Kubernetes (CKA) Certification: https://training.linuxfoundation.org/certification/certified-kubernetes-administrator-cka/

videoTitle: Cloud Engineer vs DevOps Engineer - Differences and Overlaps of tasks and responsibilities
videoDescription:
Differences and Overlaps of Cloud and DevOps Engineer Roles as well as deep dive of Cloud Engineer Tasks and Responsibilities

DevOps engineer and Cloud engineer are 2 popular roles in software engineering. But there is a lot of mix up of these two roles and many people and many companies often use them interchangeably. They mix the tasks and responsibilities of these two very often. For example there are plenty of job descriptions for "DevOps Cloud Engineer" or "Cloud DevOps Engineer", which would naturally make you think that they are the same role, but then why would you have two names for the same role? And that's exactly what I'm going to discuss and clarify in this video.


🔗💡  What is DevOps and DevOps Engineer Tasks and Responsibilities

videoTitle: What is Platform Engineering and how it fits into DevOps and Cloud world
videoDescription:
Platform Engineering - What it is, how it works and how it fits into DevOps and Cloud world
Also how to approach implementing an IDP - internal developer platform.

🔗💚   Become a DevOps Engineer in 6 months       ►   https://bit.ly/3WKUPGf
🔗💙   Become a Kubernetes Administrator              ►   https://bit.ly/3IMJUWI
🔗🧡   Complete GitLab CI/CD course                        ►   https://bit.ly/42itb4B
🔗 💜  DevSecOps Bootcamp                                       ►   https://bit.ly/3OI6y66

▬▬▬▬▬▬ Thank you Pulumi for sponsoring this video 👏  ▬▬▬▬▬▬
►   Learn more about Pulumi's Internal developer platform-in-a-box: https://pulumip.us/IDP-Platform-Engineering
►   Join PulumiUp - a global Infrastructure as Code conference: https://www.pulumi.com/pulumi-up/

In this video we're going to talk about a relatively new hot topic in DevOps and Cloud space, which is “platform engineering”. There is a lot of discussion going on, where some people are asking whether platform engineering replaces DevOps. Many people say it goes hand in hand and is rather an addition to DevOps, but in reality it's a bit more complicated than that. Platform engineering actually changes a lot of established rules we knew about DevOps, SRE and Cloud engineering, it changes the game and introduces some new rules.

So let's clearly define, what platform engineering exactly is, more interestingly, why was there even a need for this new role and how it evolved and of course how it compares to DevOps and Cloud engineering and does it really replace any of these roles?


▬▬▬▬▬▬ T I M E S T A M P S ⏰  ▬▬▬▬▬▬

17:53 - How it looks in real-world?
22:33 - Flexibility with Infrastructure as Code
24:30 - How to implement an IDP successfully
30:14 - Platform Engineering vs DevOps
34:53 - Both Skills in TWN education
39:33 - Platform vs Cloud Engineer
41:01 - Wrap Up

videoTitle: Entendendo "Devops" para Iniciantes em Programação (Parte 1) | Série "Começando aos 40"
videoDescription:
Finalmente chegamos no tema final da série! Vamos falar um pouco sobre o tal do "devops".

Esta parte meio que depende dos conceitos que vimos nos últimos episódios então se você é iniciante, e ainda não assistiu os anteriores, recomendo que faça isso.

No episódio de hoje quero dar um pouco do contexto histórico, dos anos 90 até os anos 2000, indo de configuração manual de máquinas, hospedagens compartilhadas, virtualização até os VPS. Vamos entender como uma coisa foi levando pra próxima.

Muita gente confunde muitos termos, virtualização, paravirtualização, jails, containers, hypervisors e não sabe o que é o que, então eu vou distinguir tudo pra vocês finalmente entenderem.

videoTags: what is devops in simple english
channelDescription:
GitHub is the AI-powered developer platform to build, scale, and deliver secure software. We make it easier for developers to be developers: to work together, to solve challenging problems, and to create the world’s most important technologies. We foster a collaborative community that can come together—as individuals and in teams—to create the future of software and make a difference in the world.

The GitHub platform is where developers can create, share, and ship the best code possible. Our community is made up of students, hobbyists, consultants, enterprise professionals, partners, and executives, building software in the way that works best for them.

GitHub is much more than code. It is the home of open-source collaboration. It is where new developers get started and where experienced developers expand their knowledge. It is a community where developers come together to solve the unsolvable and test the limits of what software can do.

videoTitle: How AI is Changing DevOps Careers | What You Need to Know
videoDescription:
DevOps Career in 2026: Should You Learn AI or Double Down on DevOps Fundamentals?
► Grab your DevOps Starter Kit: https://bit.ly/4oZ4qGD
► Skills Companies ACTUALLY Want: https://youtu.be/7d3MU2DgUPg

In this video I sat down with Bret Fisher - Docker Captain and DevOps educator who spent 2 years walking around conferences asking engineers one question: Are you actually using AI in DevOps?

The disconnect between what people say online and what's really happening will surprise you.

We break down:
- the AI hype vs reality
- what AI can actually do for DevOps RIGHT NOW (not in 5 or 10 years)
- and the practical first steps you can take without getting overwhelmed by the chaos

Plus: why companies still want Kubernetes experts, not AI prompt engineers.
If you're confused about AI in DevOps, this conversation will clear everything up.

Those new to the Programming and DevOps world often find it challenging to start their journey due to the missing basic prerequisites. For example, issues with setting up a basic lab environment using VirtualBox, problems with networking, or trouble with working with the Linux CLI or text editors like vi editor. Sometimes there are issues with applications - like getting a sample application to work, problems with getting the dependencies to install correctly or trouble with getting a web server to communicate with a database server. Or, at other times, it's working with data formats like JSON or YAML.

So we have identified a gap, and we believe that this is due to missing some of the basic prerequisites, such as knowing the basics of Linux, the basics of tools like VirtualBox, or knowing the basics of the most commonly used applications like Java, Python or NodeJS or web servers like Apache, NGINX etc. As a DevOps or Cloud Engineer, it is crucial to have these basics cleared. And that’s why we are helping you to bridge that gap!

videoTitle: The Future of DevOps: AI, Agents, and the "Impossible" Workflow

videoTitle: Introducing AWS DevOps Agent | Amazon Web Services
videoDescription:
AWS DevOps Agent is a frontier agent that resolves and proactively prevents incidents, optimizes application reliability and performance, and handles on-demand SRE tasks across AWS, multicloud, and on-prem environments. It investigates incidents  and identifies operational improvements as an experienced DevOps engineer would: by learning your applications and their relationships, working with your observability tools, runbooks, code repositories, and CI/CD pipelines,  and correlating telemetry, code, and deployment data across all of them. Ask questions, get instant contextual answers, and create custom charts and reports that you can save and share with your team.

videoTitle: DevOps Project: Mastering VPC Creation with Terraform & GitHub Actions Automation
videoDescription:
► ▬▬▬▬▬▬ About🧾 ▬▬▬▬▬▬
In this tutorial video, we'll walk you through creating a Virtual Private Cloud (VPC) infrastructure using Terraform and then automating the deployment of your Terraform code using GitHub Actions. Whether you're new to infrastructure as code (IAC) or looking to streamline your deployment workflow, this video covers all of it.🚀

Here's what you can expect to learn:
1. Setting up Terraform: We'll guide you through the initial setup of Terraform on your local machine, ensuring you have the necessary tools and configurations in place.🛠️

2. Writing Terraform Code: You'll see how to write Terraform code to define your VPC infrastructure, including networks, subnets, security groups, and more. We'll provide clear explanations and best practices along the way.📝

3. Initializing and Applying Terraform: Learn how to initialize your Terraform project and apply your code to create the VPC in your cloud provider.🌟

4. GitHub Repository Setup: We'll guide you through setting up a GitHub repository to store your Terraform code, including creating a README, .gitignore, and other essential files.📂

5. GitHub Actions Configuration: Discover how to configure GitHub Actions workflows to automate the deployment of your Terraform code. We'll cover secrets management and workflow triggers on pushing the code to a specific branch.⚙️

6. Push to GitHub: See how to push your Terraform code to your GitHub repository and trigger the GitHub Actions workflow.📦

7. Monitor and Verify: Learn how to monitor the progress of your GitHub Actions workflow and verify the successful deployment of your VPC infrastructure.🧐

By the end of this video, you'll have a solid understanding of how to create and manage infrastructure using Terraform and how to automate the deployment process with GitHub Actions. Don't forget to like, subscribe, and hit the bell icon to stay updated with more exciting tutorials and tech content. Let's dive in and empower your infrastructure automation journey!💪📺

•Promotes agility in your business.

videoTitle: What is Azure DevOps? | How to Use Azure DevOps
videoDescription:
Are you looking to understand how to use end-to-end solutions on Azure to implement DevOps practices throughout application planning, development, delivery, and operations? Apply the right combination of DevOps technologies, culture, and processes to enable continual software delivery and better value for customers? Azure DevOps has robust integrations with Azure, and a comprehensive suite of technologies that help you deliver software securely and quickly. Combine Azure DevOps with open-source DevOps tools to match your unique workflow, then seamlessly integrate them on Azure. Whether your app uses virtual machines, web apps, or Kubernetes, implement DevOps practices like CI/CD, infrastructure as code, and continuous monitoring with Azure and the DevOps toolchain of your choice. Want to know more about Azure DevOps?

ร่วมฟังความคิดเห็นจาก 2 มุม 2 สายอาชีพ 👨🏻‍💼 ตัวแทนทีม Software Developer โดยคุณปุ๋ย สมเกียรติ ปุ๋ยสูงเนิน - Technical Coach ที่สยามชำนาญกิจ  👨🏻‍💼 และตัวแทนทีม Operation หรือ DevOps Engineer, SRE, Platform Engineer โดยคุณเดียร์ จิรายุส นิ่มแสง - ผู้ก่อตั้ง และ CEO ของ Opsta (Thailand)

AWS re:Invent is a learning conference for the global cloud computing community. The event features keynote announcements, training and certification opportunities, access to technical sessions, deep-dives with AWS leaders, engaging with sponsors, and so much more.

AWS re:Inforce is a two day conference focused on cloud security. Attendees can expect two days of learning, with sessions on security, identity, and compliance. 

AWS Summits is a series of events that bring the cloud computing community together to connect, collaborate, and learn about AWS.

videoTitle: How to Create a CI/CD Pipeline in Azure DevOps
videoDescription:
In this video, you’ll learn how to build a CI/CD pipeline in Azure DevOps from scratch! This tutorial will guide you through the entire process, helping you set up continuous integration and continuous delivery to automate your software development workflow.

This video has everything you need, whether you're new to DevOps or looking to refine your pipeline setup.

Timestamps:

00:00 – Introduction: What is CI/CD and why it's important
03:10 – Setting Up a Project in Azure DevOps: Step-by-step guide to creating a new project
07:40 – Connecting to a Git Repository: Version control setup with Git in Azure Repos
12:25 – Creating the Build Pipeline (CI): Configuring and automating builds
20:50 – Running Tests in the Pipeline: Integrating automated tests in your CI pipeline
27:30 – Creating the Release Pipeline (CD): Automating deployments for continuous delivery
35:15 – Deploying to Azure: Connecting your pipeline to deploy your application to Azure
41:50 – Monitoring and Managing Pipelines: Best practices for monitoring and troubleshooting
48:30 – Best Practices for CI/CD Pipelines: Tips to optimize your DevOps pipeline
54:10 – Conclusion: Final thoughts and next steps
By the end of this video, you'll have a fully functional CI/CD pipeline up and running in Azure DevOps, ready to streamline your development and deployment process!

videoTitle: What is the difference between DevOps and Platform Engineering?
videoDescription:
Confused about the disparities between DevOps and Platform Engineering? Wondering if Platform Engineering is just a rebranding of DevOps? In this video, we delve into the nuances and distinctions between these two critical disciplines. Discover how DevOps began and it's impact on end-to-end application deployment, and the emmergence of Platform Engineering and it's focus on building self-service toolchains and workflows for efficient software engineering in the cloud-native era. Learn why understanding these differences is essential for successful digital transformations.

videoTitle: Understand DevOps in Under 8 Minutes | #devops | Matrix Bricks
videoDescription:
DevOps is one of the most talked-about practices in tech today, but many still aren’t clear on what it actually means. In this video, Mehul Brahmbhatt, Founder and CEO of Matrix Bricks, explains DevOps in simple, practical terms for anyone new to the concept.
Whether you're a developer, product manager, or just curious about how modern software teams work, this video covers the fundamentals in under 8 minutes.

💡 Why This Video Is a Must-Watch:
⦿ Clearly explains what DevOps is and why it exists
⦿ Helps you understand how development and operations work better together
⦿ Breaks down the major challenges DevOps solves in software delivery
⦿ Covers essential principles like automation, CI/CD, and continuous feedback

videoTitle: No coding to DevOps Career: Market Trends, Salary Insights & DevOps Job Opportunities in Germany.
videoDescription:
Are you considering a career in DevOps or exploring new opportunities in the field? In this video, we dive deep into the current DevOps job market, explore salary expectations, and discuss how learning German can significantly boost your chances of landing a job in Germany.

What You’ll Learn:

Free and Paid Learning Resources: Discover where you can start learning DevOps, from free YouTube tutorials to paid courses.
Job Market Trends: Understand the current job market for DevOps professionals in India and Germany.
Salary Expectations: Get insights on expected salaries for fresh graduates and experienced professionals, including potential earnings in startups, medium-sized companies, and large corporations.
Application Tips: Learn how to prepare a compelling resume, the importance of cover letters, and strategies for applying to job portals and company career sites.
Motivation Matters: Find out why showing genuine interest and motivation can make a difference in your job application.
Key Takeaways:

DevOps offers numerous career opportunities, even without a formal background in the field.
Learning German can give you a significant edge in the German job market.
Salary expectations vary based on experience and company size, with high earning potential in large firms and consulting companies.
Subscribe for more insights into career development, industry trends, and tips for success. Share your questions and thoughts in the comments below!

videoTitle: C'est quoi le DevOps ? Tout comprendre en deux minutes ! CI/CD, feedback et qualité !
videoDescription:
De manière triviale, le DevOps est la contraction de développement et d'opérationnel. 

Au délà d'être une méthode, c'est avant tout une culture de l'amélioration continue ancrée dans les pratiques agiles. 

Dans cette vidéo César Mourot, le cofondateur de Code Insider vous explique le DevOps et tout les aspects qui le compose.

00:00 La contraction de Dev et opérationnel 
00:18 Une culture de l'amélioration continue
00:31 Le CI/CD Continuous integration / Continuous Deployement
00:51 Des feedbacks plus rapides
01:01 Une approche autour de la qualité
01:26 Une manière opérationnelle de rendre compte d'améliorations continues
01:59 La mise en place des méthodes agiles au quotidien
02:10 Prochaine vidéo

This session explores how generative AI can revolutionize DevOps practices, accelerating deployment frequency, reducing lead time for changes, minimizing service disruptions, and mitigating change failure rates. videoTitle: He creado el OpenClaw DevOps: gestiona tu VPS, despliegues y mucho más (Open Source)
videoDescription:
Descubre OpenClaw DevOps, mi nuevo proyecto open source que revoluciona la gestión de VPS y despliegues. Olvídate de los dashboards tradicionales: OpenClaw está diseñado para que agentes de IA gestionen tu infraestructura y despliegues web de forma autónoma, desde la creación hasta la puesta en producción, todo a través de Telegram. Prepárate para una automatización DevOps sin precedentes.

¡Estoy muy emocionado de presentaros OpenClaw! Un self-hosted cloud pensado por y para la automatización con agentes, que agrupa y simplifica la gestión de toda la infraestructura necesaria para tus proyectos. Desde la creación automática de webs y apps, hasta la configuración de CI/CD, monitoreo, analíticas y backups, todo orquestado desde un agente con el que interactúas por Telegram o una CLI acotada.

En este video te explico cómo funciona, sus principales flujos de trabajo, la arquitectura que lo hace robusto y seguro, y por qué creo que es la evolución que necesitamos en la gestión de infraestructura para el desarrollo moderno. ¡Entra, pruébalo y dame tu feedback!

Si te ha gustado el proyecto y quieres apoyar el desarrollo open source, ¡no olvides darle a 'Me Gusta', suscribirte al canal para más contenido como este y dejar un comentario con tus impresiones o sugerencias!

---
LINKS ÚTILES:
Repositorio OpenClaw (GitHub): https://github.com/mutonby/pleng
Sitio Web Oficial: https://pleng.dev/
---

0:07 El Problema y la Solución
0:39 Instalación y Primeros Pasos
0:50 Flujos de Trabajo Principales
1:59 El Panel de Control y Métricas
2:36 OpenClaw es Open Source
2:58 Herramientas Integradas de DevOps
4:07 Arquitectura de Seguridad (Planks)
4:59 Comparativa y Ventajas de OpenClaw

channelDescription:
Soy Juan Carlos Cavero Gracia. En este canal comparto automatizaciones reales con n8n y IA aplicada. Nada de “suscríbete a mi skool” ni cursos eternos: subo los workflows completos para que los copies, adaptes y lances en minutos. Si te sirven, sígueme y listo.

Qué vas a ver aquí:
- Workflows n8n listos para producción (marketing, social, scraping, operaciones).
- Integraciones con Upload-Post para publicar en redes desde APIs y no-code.
- Casos prácticos de IA (agentes, prompts útiles, pipelines de datos).
- Trucos de automatización low-cost y despliegues en la vida real.
- Postmortems: qué rompí, cómo lo arreglé y qué aprendí.

Sobre mí:
- Creador de Mercadonia, la IA de Mercadona (proyectos prácticos con datos reales).
- Co-Fundador de Upload-Post.com, API para subir contenido a TikTok, Instagram, YouTube, X, etc.
- Dev/automation nerd con obsesión por cosas que ahorran tiempo y generan resultado.

Si te va lo práctico y sin postureo, este es tu sitio.

videoTitle: What Is DevOps?  - Explained With Real World Examples!
videoDescription:
Start your DevOps Career (Beginners - Advanced) - https://bit.ly/DevOpsLearningPath-YT

DevOps is a buzzword. It’s huge right now in the tech community, and it’s definitely becoming more central to how teams build software.
If you’re starting with DevOps, this video will help you understand the basics and give you a good head-start on your journey. You will also learn about the different stages in DevOps, what tools are needed at each stage, and how potential problems should be handled.

Start your DevOps Career (Beginners - Advanced) - https://bit.ly/DevOpsLearningPath-YT

In this video we will cover the following topics:

00:00 -  Introduction
00:27 - Problems before DevOps
01:33 - Two Stages of DevOps
02:12 - Real World Scenario 1
03:28 - Real World Scenario 2
04:12 - Automation
05:06 - Benefits of DevOps
06:10 - Summary
07:22 - Conclusion
 
Those new to the Programming and DevOps world often find it challenging to start their journey due to the missing basic prerequisites. For example, issues with setting up a basic lab environment using VirtualBox, problems with networking, or trouble with working with the Linux CLI or text editors like vi editor. Sometimes there are issues with applications - like getting a sample application to work, problems with getting the dependencies to install correctly or trouble with getting a web server to communicate with a database server. Or, at other times, it's working with data formats like JSON or YAML.

So we have identified a gap, and we believe that this is due to missing some of the basic prerequisites, such as knowing the basics of Linux, the basics of tools like VirtualBox, or knowing the basics of the most commonly used applications like Java, Python or NodeJS or web servers like Apache, NGINX etc. As a DevOps or Cloud Engineer, it is crucial to have these basics cleared. And that’s why we are helping you to bridge that gap!

Want to view a clear path to becoming a DevOps Engineer? Visit our website kodekloud.com!
We have various Learning Paths to help you choose your next step and shape your DevOps Career. 

Check out our learning paths at KodeKloud to get started:

videoTitle: هل الـ DevOps engineers مطلوبين في الشركات؟
videoDescription: في الحلقة دي بنناقش كل التفاصيل المهمة عن عالم DevOps. هنتكلم عن اللي كان بيحصل في تطوير البرمجيات قبل ظهور الـ DevOps وإزاي غير المشهد تمامًا. كمان هنوضح الفرق بين DevOps وSRE، وليه بعض الشركات بتفضل تستخدم واحدة عن التانية. لو بتسأل عن فرص الشغل في المجال ده سواء في مصر أو بره، هتعرف حجم الفرص وأهميتها ومتوسط الرواتب اللي ممكن تحققها. الحلقة كمان بتجاوب على سؤال مهم: إيه اللي محتاج تعمله كخريج جديد عشان تبدأ في المجال؟ وهتديك نصايح عملية لكل اللي محتاج تكون مجهزه قبل ما تروح الإنترفيو. متفوتش الفرصة دي لو عايز تبدأ مشوارك في DevOps بخطوات ثابتة وواضحة.

videoTitle: DKT88 - DevOps Mock interview #6 (Senior/Lead DevOps engineer)
videoDescription:
Очередное мок-собеседование на канале! Сегодня в гостях Саша (не СТО) — Senior DevOps Engineer, с которым мы проходим по всему циклу разработки: от проектирования CI/CD до траблшутинга в Kubernetes. 
Много практики, лайв-кодинга и архитектурных вопросов.
Слушаем, запоминаем вопросы, влетаем за разбором на Patreon/Бусти!

🔍 ЧТО СПРАШИВАЛИ

- CI/CD: проектируем идеальный пайплайн для Java-бэкенда и спорим о порядке стейджей
- Git Internals: навигация по дереву коммитов (Detached HEAD) и правка истории
- Kubernetes: архитектура (Cluster API, Control Plane), чем на самом деле занимается Controller Manager и нюансы StatefulSet
- Troubleshooting: чиним права доступа (RBAC) в кластере и разбираем упавший Docker-билд с пропавшими пакетами
- Helm & IaC: зачем нужны Library charts, шаблонизация и структура Terraform-репозитория

videoTitle: Intro to DevOps | AZ-400 | Episode 1
videoDescription:
Episode 1 of 25
For the full video series, click here: https://aka.ms/AZ-400onYouTube

In this episode, Microsoft Technical Trainer Andrew Conniff introduces Module 01: Introduction to DevOps. You’ll explore DevOps as the union of people, process, and products to enable continuous delivery of value, and learn why continuous improvement and cycle-time optimization are essential in modern software delivery.

The session covers the fundamentals of Git repositories, branching workflows, pull requests, collaboration, and code review. You’ll also examine key DevOps practices, including CI/CD, monitoring and logging, cloud fundamentals, infrastructure as code, microservices, and containers. The episode concludes with an overview of Azure DevOps (Boards, Pipelines, Repos, Artifacts, Test Plans) and GitHub (Repositories, Codespaces, Actions, Packages, and security features) and how they support end-to-end DevOps workflows aligned to AZ-400 certification objectives.

Learn more about this course and take the certification exam to test your new skills: https://aka.ms/AZ-400onLearn

videoTitle: Open Source Cloud: Host and Deploy OSS, No DevOps
videoDescription:
Reusing proven open source beats regenerating the same code with AI over and over. It is faster, cheaper on compute, and you are not reinventing the wheel every time. Open Source Cloud is where Eyevinn hosts open source solutions so you can run them without standing up your own infrastructure.

What you can do with it:

Start a hosted service, like a database instance, from the web UI and use it right away
Connect Open Source Cloud to your AI tools (Claude Code, Codex) as a connector and build against real running services
Deploy instantly. Your code, open source, no lock-in, online immediately
As an example, our CTO Jonas built a full streaming service in a single weekend using agentic engineering on top of Open Source Cloud. A year ago the same build would have taken him months, even at his level.

If you run open source services and you are tired of the DevOps overhead between "this works locally" and "this is deployed," this is the workflow worth a look.

Start here: https://www.osaas.io?utm_source=youtube&utm_medium=social&u

Start here: https://www.osaas.io?utm_source=youtube&utm_medium=social&utm_campaign=osc-open-source-cloud

37:27 → Part 7: Configuring VM Authentication
37:58 → Creating an Azure App Registration
38:48 → Running the Self-Signed Certificate PowerShell Script
39:30 → Running the Admin User Provisioning Tool
40:14 → Accessing F&O Locally via IIS
41:29 → Recap

──────────────
💡 What You'll Learn
──────────────

✓ How to provision a new Lifecycle Services (LCS) implementation project.
✓ Configure Azure DevOps for D365 (including TFVC & Classic Pipeline settings).
✓ The critical step of connecting LCS to Azure DevOps for integration.
✓ How to deploy and access a local development VHD using Hyper-V.
✓ Authenticate the local VM using Azure App Registration.

──────────────────────────
🚀 About This D365 Development Series
──────────────────────────

This series goes beyond basic tutorials to show you how real-world Dynamics 365 (D365FO) projects are built from start to finish. We'll build a complete, end-to-end "Automated Customer Tiering System" for a fictional client, Bluestone Industrial Components (BIC).

You'll see every step of a production-ready solution, from setup to deployment, covering:
➤ Core X++ Development: Extending tables and forms (CustTable), creating Enums, and implementing custom business logic using Chain of Command (CoC).
➤ Automation: Building robust, automated batch jobs with the SysOperation Framework.
➤ APIs & Integration: Creating Data Entities to build secure OData REST APIs and for Data Management Framework (DMF) exports.
➤ Event-Driven Architecture: Firing real-time alerts from D365 to Azure Logic Apps using Business Events.
➤ Business Intelligence: Connecting D365 to Power BI to build an interactive sales dashboard.
➤ DevOps & ALM: Setting up a complete CI/CD pipeline in Azure DevOps, managing source control (TFVC), and automating builds.

────────────────
💻 Tools & Technologies
────────────────

🔹Dynamics 365 Finance & Operations (D365FO)
🔹X++
🔹Visual Studio 2019
🔹Lifecycle Services (LCS)
🔹Azure DevOps (ADO)
🔹SQL Server Management Studio (SSMS)
🔹Power BI Desktop
🔹Azure Logic Apps

───────────────────
💬 Feedback & Future Topics
───────────────────

This series covers the core components of a practical, end-to-end D365 project. I know there are many more topics we could dive into, from advanced debugging to complex form patterns.

I welcome your feedback and ideas! If there's a specific D365 topic you'd like to see covered in a future video, please share your suggestions in the comments section below.

#Dynamics365, #D365Development, #LifecycleServices, #AzureDevOps

videoTitle: DKT97 | DevOps в 2026: Platform Engineering, AI-агенты и будущее джунов
videoDescription:
Состояние DevOps в 2026: Platform Engineering, AI-агенты и что стало с junior-инженерами. Собрались на кухне с тимлидом системного юнита из большой компании - поговорить что и как сейча.

О ЧЁМ ВЫПУСК
• DevOps vs SRE в 2026: где проходит граница и почему «you build it, you run it» иногда создаёт больше проблем, чем решает.
• War story: упавший Kubernetes во время корпоратива с пивом - классика первых K8s-внедрений.
• Момент, когда DevOps ломается: 600 сервисов, 3600 пайплайнов и почему каждый новый инженер пишет 3601-й.
• Platform Engineering: зачем нужна платформа, что такое метаплатформы и как устроены слои внутри крупной компании.
• Junior + AI = middle: что изменилось с приходом AI-ассистентов и сколько теперь занимает обучение DevOps.
• AI в работе DevOps прямо сейчас: мультиагентные помощники для расследования инцидентов, внутренние vs внешние модели.
• Реальные AI-катастрофы 2025-2026: Replit дропнул базу и бэкапы, сервис аренды машин ушатал прод.
• Multi-agent flow: refiner + архитектор + автономный бот, PR за час вместо недели.
• Тимлидам: не носи инженерам PR, которые ты навайпкодил за вечер.
• Что реально учить в 2026: Linux, сеть, Kubernetes, один язык программирования и AI-грамотность.
• Знать базу vs спрашивать AI: почему без фундамента ты не поймёшь, куда тебя модель направляет.

ГОСТЬ
В гостях - Андрей Волхонский, руководитель юнита System в Центре разработки инфраструктуры Авито. 13+ лет опыта: от Windows-DevOps в TravelLine и Kaspersky до платформенной инженерии в большой продуктовой компании.

ССЫЛКИ
💰 Поддержать наш подкаст:
  - Patreon - https://www.patreon.com/c/devopskitchentalks
  - Boosty - https://boosty.to/devopskitchentalks

НАВИГАЦИЯ

Lars Kruse - Technology Counselor & Rainmaker at Inc Inc
Malte Foegen - COO at wibas
Klaus Bucka-Lassen - Free Radical at Netcetera & Agile Coach, Trainer & Keynote Speaker @danish.kauboy 

DESCRIPTION
Agile, Lean, and DevOps are more than buzzwords even though they have taken over the world at different times. The processes and technologies they employed have helped improve the entire world, not just the software world. Klaus Bucka-Lassen, Lars Kruse, and Malte Foegen debate the intersection and cross-pollination between the three worlds with a focus on applying them on all levels in practice in large organizations.

videoTitle: DKT93 - DevOps Mock Interview: Terraform, K8s, AI и Live Coding
videoDescription:
Думаете, знаете полный lifecycle удаления пода в Kubernetes? Даже опытный инженер с CKA запнулся на этом вопросе. Мок-собеседование на Senior DevOps с live coding и тремя интервьюерами.

ЧТО СПРАШИВАЛИ
- Cloud: миграция AWS в Yandex Cloud, DynamoDB в YDB, Kafka HA в трех зонах
- Terraform: структура репозитория, Terragrunt, state management, code review модулей
- Kubernetes: pod lifecycle, graceful shutdown, kube-proxy, endpoint slices
- Live coding: Helm troubleshooting сломанного StatefulSet upgrade
- AI/LLM: токенизация, промпт-инженерия, MCP Server - "обычно говоришь LLM сделай красиво"
- CI/CD: pipeline от merge request до prod, rolling update vs canary, GitOps и Argo CD
- Скриптинг: code review скрипта удаления Docker-образов из registry

КАНДИДАТ
Владимир Ушков - инфраструктурный DevOps-инженер с опытом в AWS, Yandex Cloud, Terraform, Kubernetes, CI/CD.

Интересный момент: кандидат честно признался, что цифра "улучшил на 23%" в CV была "органолептической" - и добавил: "в эпоху LLM резюме тоже надо писать для LLM".

Фидбэк по интервью доступен на Patreon/Boosty.

videoTitle: DevOps vs. SRE
videoDescription:
What is the difference between DevOps and SRE? 

In Short, DevOps should be an all-encompassing term for connecting the development team and operations team. However, DevOps tends to focus more on Deployment, whereas SRE focuses on Reliability.

videoTitle: DevOps in 2026: Stay in DevOps or Transition to MLOps, AIOps, DataOps?
videoDescription:
As we close out 2025, many DevOps engineers are facing the same question:
Should I continue growing in DevOps, or is it time to transition into MLOps, AIOps, or DataOps?

With AI agents and agentic workflows increasingly automating infrastructure and operations, it’s natural to wonder how these changes impact DevOps roles and career paths going into 2026.

In this video, I break down what’s actually changing — and what isn’t. You’ll get a clear, practical explanation of DevOps, MLOps, AIOps, and DataOps, how AI agents fit across all of these domains, and how to think about your career without chasing hype or fear-driven narratives.

What you’ll learn in this video:

Why DevOps is not dying, but evolving into specialized domains

The real difference between DevOps, MLOps, AIOps, and DataOps

Where AI agents and agentic workflows actually fit in operations

When it makes sense to stay in DevOps

When transitioning to MLOps, AIOps, or DataOps is the right move

Why these paths are not mutually exclusive

How to choose a direction based on the problems that genuinely excite you

This video is designed for engineers who want clarity, not clickbait — and who want to make career decisions grounded in real systems, real demand, and real interest.

If this helped you understand where the industry is heading, consider subscribing. In upcoming videos, I’ll go deeper into each specialization — DevOps, MLOps, AIOps, and DataOps — with real-world context and practical insights.

#DevOps #MLOps #AIOps #DataOps #DevOpsCareer #AIAgents #AgenticWorkflows #CloudEngineering #FutureOfDevOps

videoTitle: Docker MCP Tutorial for DevOps Alerts (2026) | What is MCP? | MCP Servers
videoDescription:

🖥️ Download Docker Desktop here: https://dockr.ly/4aXj904

🎓 What is MCP and how do MCP servers work? This MCP tutorial walks you through using MCP servers with Docker Desktop to let AI investigate your DevOps alerts automatically, covering Kubernetes, Grafana, Prometheus, and the Docker MCP Toolkit gateway explained with simple visuals.

In this MCP for DevOps guide, you'll see:
How a real hoodie store app deployed in Kubernetes generates metrics, logs, and alerts with OpenTelemetry
How Prometheus, Loki, and Grafana run as containers inside Docker Desktop to monitor everything
What MCP is, how MCP servers and MCP clients work together, and why the standard matters
How the Docker MCP gateway lets you add multiple MCP servers through one single integration instead of configuring each one manually

If you want AI to troubleshoot your alerts using Kubernetes and Grafana MCP servers, this is the place to start.

videoTitle: GitHub vs GitLab: Which DevOps Platform is Best for Your Project?
videoDescription:
The ultimate fight for the modern developer workflow comes down to two giants: GitHub and GitLab. They both start with Git, but their fundamental philosophies on how you build, test, and deploy software couldn't be more different. Are you better off with GitHub's flexible "Best-of-Breed" Marketplace approach, or is GitLab's unified, "All-in-One" DevSecOps platform the right choice for your enterprise?

In this comprehensive comparison, I break down the core features, CI/CD platforms (GitHub Actions vs. GitLab CI/CD), security models, and hosting flexibility to help you make the definitive decision for your team, whether you're working on open-source, a complex enterprise pipeline, or a heavily regulated project.

Watch the full video to find out which platform is the absolute best fit for your specific needs!

What do you think? Which platform are you using for your current project and why? Let me know in the comments below!

If you enjoy the video,  like it, and subscribe to my channel!
See you in the next video!

videoTitle: D365 F&O: Setup an Azure DevOps CI Build Pipeline | Part 7
videoDescription:
Set up a CI pipeline for D365 in Azure DevOps. Learn to deploy a Build VM and automate your code builds for team development.

───────────
🕒 Timestamps
───────────

0:00 → Intro: Why We Need a CI Pipeline (Continuous Integration)
0:25 → Part 1: Create a Self-Hosted Agent Pool in ADO
1:19 → Part 2: Provision the Build VM in LCS
1:54 → Selecting the "Build and Test" Topology
2:24 → Advanced Settings: Linking the ADO Agent Pool & Branch
3:04 → Selecting the VM Size (Memory Optimized)
3:47 → Part 3: Configure the Build VM (Reset Password & Connect)
4:24 → Exploring the Build VM File Structure (SDK Folder)
4:53 → Part 4: Review the Auto-Generated Build Pipeline
5:43 → Enabling Continuous Integration Triggers & Path Filters
6:23 → Part 5: Branching Strategy (Rename 'dev' to 'Main')
6:52 → Connecting Visual Studio to Source Control (Team Explorer)
7:19 → Mapping a Local Workspace
7:55 → Adding the Local Project to Source Control
8:34 → Adding the axmodulesbuild File & Checking In
9:12 → Part 6: Creating a New 'dev' Branch from 'Main'
10:06 → Part 7: How the CI Build Process Works
10:23 → Reviewing the Build Stages (Build, Sync, Package)
10:59 → Under the Hood: Pipeline Tasks vs. VM Scripts
11:53 → Part 8: Reviewing the Final Build Artifact
12:27 → Recap

──────────────
💡 What You'll Learn
──────────────

✓ Create a self-hosted Agent Pool in Azure DevOps for D365 builds.
✓ How to deploy a Build VM from LCS and connect it to your pipeline.
✓ Configure an auto-generated build pipeline with CI triggers and path filters.
✓ Connect Visual Studio to Azure DevOps (TFVC) using Team Explorer.
✓ Check in your code to automatically trigger the build and create a Deployable Package.

──────────────────────────
🚀 About This D365 Development Series
──────────────────────────

This series goes beyond basic tutorials to show you how real-world Dynamics 365 (D365FO) projects are built from start to finish. We'll build a complete, end-to-end "Automated Customer Tiering System" for a fictional client, Bluestone Industrial Components (BIC).

You'll see every step of a production-ready solution, from setup to deployment, covering:
➤ Core X++ Development: Extending tables and forms (CustTable), creating Enums, and implementing custom business logic using Chain of Command (CoC).
➤ Automation: Building robust, automated batch jobs with the SysOperation Framework.
➤ APIs & Integration: Creating Data Entities to build secure OData REST APIs and for Data Management Framework (DMF) exports.
➤ Event-Driven Architecture: Firing real-time alerts from D365 to Azure Logic Apps using Business Events.
➤ Business Intelligence: Connecting D365 to Power BI to build an interactive sales dashboard.
➤ DevOps & ALM: Setting up a complete CI/CD pipeline in Azure DevOps, managing source control (TFVC), and automating builds.

────────────────
💻 Tools & Technologies
────────────────

🔹Dynamics 365 Finance & Operations (D365FO)
🔹X++
🔹Visual Studio 2019
🔹Lifecycle Services (LCS)
🔹Azure DevOps (ADO)
🔹SQL Server Management Studio (SSMS)
🔹Power BI Desktop
🔹Azure Logic Apps

───────────────────
💬 Feedback & Future Topics
───────────────────

This series covers the core components of a practical, end-to-end D365 project. I know there are many more topics we could dive into, from advanced debugging to complex form patterns.

I welcome your feedback and ideas! If there's a specific D365 topic you'd like to see covered in a future video, please share your suggestions in the comments section below.

#Dynamics365, #AzureDevOps, #ContinuousIntegration, #D365Development

videoTitle: Authentic DevOps with AI Foundry + GitHub, on Sip & Sync with Azure
videoDescription:
Join us for another exciting episode of Sip and Sync with Azure! ☁️🎤 Host Katie Novotny welcomes Krystal Folkes, Software Global Black Belt at Microsoft, to explore how Azure AI Foundry agents can revolutionize DevOps workflows by integrating with GitHub and GitHub Copilot.
In this hands-on demo, Krystal walks through building a security-focused agent using MCP tools, connecting to GitHub's advanced security advisories, and orchestrating multi-agent workflows—all from a local machine. Whether you're a DevOps engineer, security lead, or developer curious about AI-powered automation, this episode is packed with insights to elevate your developer experience.

What You'll Learn:

- How to build a security agent using Azure AI Foundry
- Connecting GitHub MCP tools to automate vulnerability insights
- Using GitHub Copilot to fine-tune prompts for agent instructions
- Running agents locally with secure GitHub token authentication
- Orchestrating multi-agent workflows for DevOps, planning, and documentation
- Leveraging Azure Key Vault and managed identity for secure integrations


Chapters:
00:05 - Welcome & Introductions
00:25 - Enhancing Azure Identity Developer Experience
00:46 - What is Authentic DevOps?
01:02 - Connecting Azure AI Foundry Agents with GitHub
01:43 - Building a Security Agent with MCP Tools

04:28 - Prompt Engineering with GitHub Copilot
05:54 - Customizing Security Tool Access
06:54 - GitHub MCP Server Authorization Setup
08:03 - Running the Agent Locally
09:09 - Viewing Security Advisories in Action
10:59 - Multi-Agent Orchestration Possibilities
12:02 - Final Thoughts & Call to Action


Speakers:
Krystal Folkes – Software Global Black Belt, Microsoft
Crystal specializes in connecting Azure and GitHub technologies to create transformative developer experiences. In this episode, Crystal shares how to build intelligent agents that automate security workflows and enhance DevOps practices.

videoTitle: The DevOps Strategy Behind Stable Product Launches
videoDescription:
What if your biggest product launch becomes your biggest outage?
 
In this video, Mehul Brahmbhatt, CEO & Founder of Matrix Bricks, explains how DevOps helps businesses prevent crashes, downtime, deployment failures, and launch-day chaos.
 
From infrastructure scaling to real-time monitoring, DevOps has become one of the most important foundations for successful digital product launches.


🎯 Key Takeaways

▫️Why products fail during launch despite successful testing
▫️How DevOps prepares systems for real-world traffic pressure
▫️The role of CI/CD in reducing deployment risks
▫️Why continuous testing improves launch stability
▫️How real-time monitoring prevents major downtime
▫️Why security must be integrated before launch day
▫️How collaboration between teams improves execution


👀 Why You Should Watch This Video

▫️Learn how to prevent launch-day crashes and outages
▫️Understand how high-performing tech teams prepare for scale
▫️Discover how DevOps improves performance and stability
▫️Learn how CI/CD pipelines reduce deployment errors
▫️Understand the importance of monitoring and recovery systems
▫️Build more reliable and scalable digital products

videoTitle: Cloud Architect Portfolio: What to Build When You Have No Experience
videoDescription:
A cloud architect portfolio should prove that you can think, communicate, and make decisions like a real cloud architect.

In this video, I explain what should be in a cloud architect portfolio when you have no experience and want to get hired for your first cloud architect or solutions architect role.

Most people build the wrong portfolio. They fill it with Terraform scripts, Linux commands, automation projects, cloud labs, and GitHub repositories. That may be useful for a cloud engineer, but it does not prove you can operate as a cloud architect.

Cloud engineers build and configure technology. Cloud architects design, decide, lead, evaluate tradeoffs, reduce risk, control cost, create governance, and align technology with business outcomes.
That is why your cloud architect portfolio must show more than technical knowledge. It must show architecture thinking.

In this video, you will learn how to build a cloud architect portfolio that hiring managers actually want to see. We will cover cloud architect portfolio examples, cloud architect portfolio projects, solutions architect portfolio examples, and the difference between a cloud architect portfolio vs cloud engineer portfolio.

You will learn why your portfolio should include real architecture work products such as reference architectures, architecture decision records, business cases, as is and to be architectures, cloud migration roadmaps, governance artifacts, policies, standards, guidelines, vendor evaluations, and executive summaries.

You will also learn what hiring managers look for in a cloud architect portfolio and why a strong portfolio can change your interview. Instead of asking an employer to believe you can do the job, you can walk them through your decisions, your business context, your tradeoffs, your recommendations, and your outcomes.

If you are wondering how to create a cloud architect portfolio, how to build a cloud architect portfolio, or what belongs in a cloud architect portfolio for beginners, this video will show you the right way to do it.

The goal is not to prove that you studied cloud. The goal is to prove that you can act, think, communicate, and lead like a cloud architect.

At Go Cloud Careers, we train people to become real cloud architects, enterprise architects, security architects, and AI architects. We teach the technical foundations, but we also teach business acumen, executive communication, leadership, stakeholder management, governance, decision making, and the real skills hiring managers expect from architects.

Architects are not paid to click buttons. Architects are paid to create business outcomes.

Want to accelerate your career? Join our free live architecture webinars https://bit.ly/3Sw9iDW

Where we teach you:
• What architects really do
• The skills needed to get hired
• How to position yourself for executive-level roles
• How to stand out in competitive interviews

videoTitle: Da li je DevOps stvarno NAJPLAĆENIJI posao u IT-ju?
videoDescription:
In this episode of the Codekido series we answer the 10 most frequently asked questions about our lives as engineering professionals our career paths and the reality of the tech industry. We move beyond the code to discuss the stress the passion and the unconventional journeys that led us to DevOps.

From the origin story of the name Codekido to the debate over whether a university degree is necessary for a successful career in engineering we share our honest perspectives. We also discuss our personal backgrounds including missed opportunities in nuclear physics and medicine and how those signs led us to where we are today.

Key topics covered in this video:
The authentic story behind the name Codekido and its surprising meaning in Japanese.
Our daily routines outside of the office including gym sessions hiking and marathon study sessions for doctoral degrees.
Alternative career paths we almost took such as becoming a doctor in Germany or studying nuclear physics in Dresden.
The university vs bootcamp debate and why we believe a formal education provides a critical foundation for problem solving.
Handling the stress of the tech industry and debunking the myth that you do not need to love your job to be successful.
Why DevOps is often perceived as one of the highest paid roles and the complexity that justifies it.
The importance of believing in the process and learning from every setback on your professional journey.

Video Chapters:
00:00:00 Introduction to the most frequently asked questions
00:00:40 How the name Codekido was born and the Japanese connection
00:02:18 What we do when we are not coding fitness and hobbies

00:05:03 Do you need a university degree for a career in DevOps
00:07:05 Best and worst career advice we have ever received

videoTitle: How to Fix High CPU Usage in Production | DevOps Real-Time Scenario (Day 3)
videoDescription:
💥 Day 3 of the DevOps in ARIA Series – Real-Time Scenarios!

🎯 Today’s Interview Question:
“Your production server is showing high CPU usage — what will you do?”

This is more than an interview question — it’s a real-world DevOps challenge you’ll face on the job.

🎥 In this video, I break down:

✅ How to investigate CPU issues using tools like top, htop, Grafana, and Prometheus

✅ How to identify which process or service is consuming resources

✅ When to restart a service — and what to check before and after

✅ How code bugs or deployment issues can spike CPU

✅ When to scale infra or adjust auto-scaling policies

✅ Why documenting and learning from incidents makes you a stronger engineer

💡 Clarity, calmness, and a systematic approach — that’s the DevOps way!

📌 If you're preparing for DevOps interviews or working in production environments, this video will sharpen your troubleshooting skills.

AWS re:Invent is a learning conference for the global cloud computing community. The event features keynote announcements, training and certification opportunities, access to technical sessions, deep-dives with AWS leaders, engaging with sponsors, and so much more.

AWS re:Inforce is a two day conference focused on cloud security. Attendees can expect two days of learning, with sessions on security, identity, and compliance. 

AWS Summits is a series of events that bring the cloud computing community together to connect, collaborate, and learn about AWS. 

AWSome Day Online Conference is a free, online training event that will provide a step-by-step introduction to the core AWS services for compute, storage, databases, and networking.

videoTitle: Microsoft and Black Duck DevOps Partnership: Build Secure, High-Quality Software Faster | Black Duck
videoDescription:
Modern software development demands speed—but without integrated security, that speed increases risk. As organizations adopt cloud and DevOps practices, application security must be embedded earlier in the development lifecycle to ensure quality and resilience at scale.

In this video, explore how Black Duck application security solutions integrate with Microsoft Azure DevOps to help teams identify and remediate vulnerabilities early—enabling faster, more secure software delivery.

In this overview, you’ll learn:

- Why most cyber attacks target the application layer and how that impacts modern development
- The importance of shifting application security earlier in the SDLC
- How integrating AppSec into Azure DevOps workflows reduces risk without slowing teams down
- How Black Duck enables automated security testing across development pipelines
- How organizations can improve software quality while maintaining delivery speed

Embed security into your DevOps workflows to reduce risk and accelerate delivery across cloud‑native applications.

Learn more about the Black Duck and Microsoft partnership when you visit: https://www.blackduck.com/partners.html

videoTitle: Create Managed Azure DevOps Pools with Terraform
videoDescription:
Today we're working with Azure Pipelines and exploring the different options you have when working with agent pools. The main focus will be on the new Azure Managed DevOps Agent Pools, what they are, how they work, and why you might want to use them.

We’ll start by deploying some prerequisites using Terraform, including a virtual network to host our agents. You’ll see how to use the AzAPI provider to get the job done and I’ll walk you through it all, step by step, as we build this Terraform project from scratch.

Once everything is up and running, we’ll connect our agent to an Azure Pipeline and run a deployment to test it out.

If you’re looking to automate Azure DevOps agent pool provisioning with Terraform and AzAPI, or just want to learn more about CI/CD in Azure, you’re in the right place. Hope you enjoy the video!

Timestamps: 
0:00 Intro
1:00 Differences between managed devops pools and other offerings
3:40 Comparison table between VMSS and Managed Devops Pool
5:40 Writing our Terraform code
27:50 Writing Terraform for our Managed Devops pool
33:30 Deploying our code
34:10 Create pipelines and test our pool
38:20 Recap and outro

videoTitle: Azure DevOps Tutorial for Beginners - Full Step-by-Step Guide 2025 + Bicep AKS Deployment
videoDescription:
Azure DevOps Tutorial for Beginners [2025 Edition]

In this full step-by-step guide, you’ll learn how to set up and use Azure DevOps — from creating your first DevOps organization to deploying a Kubernetes (AKS) cluster through an automated pipeline.

Whether you’re a developer, DevOps engineer, or IT professional, this tutorial will help you master the foundations of continuous integration and continuous deployment (CI/CD) using Microsoft’s powerful DevOps platform.

What You’ll Learn in This Video:
 — What is Azure DevOps & Why Teams Use It
 — How to Create a DevOps Organization
 — How to Add a User in Azure DevOps & Assign Access Levels
 — How to Create a Group & Grant Project Permissions
 — How to Create a Service Connection (to Azure Subscription)
 — Overview of Azure DevOps Core Components (Boards, Repos, Pipelines, Test Plans, Artifacts)
 — Deploy a Kubernetes Cluster to Azure via DevOps Pipeline
 — Final Thoughts & Next Steps

After Watching This Tutorial, You’ll Be Able To:

- Set up your Azure DevOps environment from scratch
- Manage users, groups, and project permissions
- Configure a secure service connection to Azure
- Understand the main DevOps components: Boards, Repos, Pipelines, and Artifacts
- Deploy an Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS) cluster automatically using a CI/CD pipeline

videoTitle: Boosting Productivity: Mastering SSH Integration in Azure DevOps And Git Repos
videoDescription:
How to clone git repository via SSH Keys | How to clone git repository via SSH Authentication

Command: ssh-keygen -t rsa -b 4096

0:00 Introduction
0:15 How to generate SSH keys (private and public)
2:10 How to add SSH public key into Azure DevOps Organization

In this era of cloud computing and automation, mastering SSH authentication is essential for any developer. SSH or Secure Shell is a cryptographic network protocol designed for secure data communication. It is often used in passwordless authentication scenarios, improving the productivity and security of developers.

Working with Azure DevOps, an all-inclusive suite of development tools provided by Microsoft, can be significantly enhanced by using SSH. Azure DevOps comprises various services including Azure Repos, which is a set of version control tools that help developers manage their code. Azure Repos is versatile in that it supports both Git and Team Foundation Version Control.

One of the ways to interact with Azure Repos is using Git, a popular open-source version control system. When using Git with Azure Repos, developers have the flexibility to work with their codebase using various Git clients, including the popular VS Code. However, to establish a secure connection between these clients and Azure Repos, proper authentication is crucial.

This is where SSH comes in. Using SSH, you can authenticate to Azure DevOps, and particularly, Azure Git Repositories, without entering your username and password every time. This not only saves time but also minimizes the risk of your credentials being exposed.

This guide aims to help developers configure SSH for Azure DevOps, understand how to clone Azure Git Repos using SSH, and embrace passwordless and public key authentication methods for seamless and secure operations. Whether you're an IT professional, software engineer, or someone interested in learning about DevOps, this information will equip you with valuable insights into SSH integration and usage.

This video is about:-
SSH Authentication
Azure DevOps Tutorials
Azure Repos
Azure Git Repositories 
VS Code with Azure DevOps
Git Authentication
Clone Azure Git Repo with SSH
How to use SSH authentication with Azure DevOps 
Ssh passwordless authentication
Ssh with public key authentication 
Using SSH to authenticate Git in Azure DevOps
Clone git Repository using SSH in Azure DevOps

videoTitle: Steps to Enable Parallel Jobs In Azure DevOps (AZ-400)
videoDescription:
All you will get information in this Video: https://youtu.be/DBQLl360Mz0

Purpose of Parallel Jobs: The video explains how to enable parallel jobs in Azure DevOps, which is necessary for running CI/CD pipelines.

Creating a Project: Start by creating a project within your Azure DevOps organization, noting your organization URL.

Access Billing Settings: Navigate to the organization settings, select "Billing," and locate the option for parallel jobs.

Parallel Jobs Availability: Initially, new organizations have zero parallel jobs available, preventing pipeline execution until jobs are enabled.

Requesting Parallel Jobs: Users must request parallel jobs through a specific URL provided by Microsoft, as they no longer provide them by default due to past misuse.

Misuse Prevention: Microsoft stopped offering free parallel jobs by default because users exploited them for unauthorized activities like Bitcoin mining.

Request Process: Fill out the form with your name, the same email ID used for Azure DevOps, and your organization URL. Request only private parallel jobs unless you have a valid reason for public jobs.

Approval Timeframe: It typically takes 24 to 48 hours for Microsoft to approve parallel job requests.

Verification: After approval, you can verify the availability of parallel jobs in the Azure DevOps portal under the "Parallel Jobs" section in the billing settings.

Billing Setup for Additional Jobs: For additional parallel jobs, users need an active Azure subscription (e.g., Pay-As-You-Go), and setup billing, linking it to the Azure subscription. This will allow for purchasing more parallel jobs if needed.

videoTitle: AWS ECS Tutorial | Deploying Docker Containers on AWS Elastic Container Service | DevOps in Telugu
videoDescription:
AWS ECS Tutorial | Deploying Docker Containers on AWS Elastic Container Service | DevOps in Telugu

Amazon Elastic Container Service (ECS) is a fully managed container orchestration service offered by Amazon Web Services. This tutorial will cover the core concepts of ECS and provide a deep dive into how it works. Beginners will find this tutorial helpful in understanding how to deploy containers using ECS.

AWS ECS allows users to easily run, stop, and manage Docker containers on a cluster of virtual servers. The service abstracts away the complexities of managing infrastructure, making it more accessible for those new to containerization. Through this tutorial, you will learn how to set up and configure ECS services for your applications.

By following along with step-by-step instructions and demonstrations, you will gain a clearer understanding of how AWS ECS operates. Videos included in the tutorial will visually guide beginners through the processes involved in deploying containers using ECS. Overall, this comprehensive tutorial aims to equip beginners with the necessary knowledge and skills to leverage AWS ECS effectively.

videoTitle: What Is DevOps?  - Explained With Real World Examples!
videoDescription:
Start your DevOps Career (Beginners - Advanced) - https://bit.ly/DevOpsLearningPath-YT

DevOps is a buzzword. It’s huge right now in the tech community, and it’s definitely becoming more central to how teams build software.
If you’re starting with DevOps, this video will help you understand the basics and give you a good head-start on your journey. You will also learn about the different stages in DevOps, what tools are needed at each stage, and how potential problems should be handled.

Start your DevOps Career (Beginners - Advanced) - https://bit.ly/DevOpsLearningPath-YT

In this video we will cover the following topics:

00:00 -  Introduction
00:27 - Problems before DevOps
01:33 - Two Stages of DevOps
02:12 - Real World Scenario 1
03:28 - Real World Scenario 2
04:12 - Automation
05:06 - Benefits of DevOps
06:10 - Summary
07:22 - Conclusion
 
Those new to the Programming and DevOps world often find it challenging to start their journey due to the missing basic prerequisites. For example, issues with setting up a basic lab environment using VirtualBox, problems with networking, or trouble with working with the Linux CLI or text editors like vi editor. Sometimes there are issues with applications - like getting a sample application to work, problems with getting the dependencies to install correctly or trouble with getting a web server to communicate with a database server. Or, at other times, it's working with data formats like JSON or YAML.

So we have identified a gap, and we believe that this is due to missing some of the basic prerequisites, such as knowing the basics of Linux, the basics of tools like VirtualBox, or knowing the basics of the most commonly used applications like Java, Python or NodeJS or web servers like Apache, NGINX etc. As a DevOps or Cloud Engineer, it is crucial to have these basics cleared. And that’s why we are helping you to bridge that gap!

Want to view a clear path to becoming a DevOps Engineer? Visit our website kodekloud.com!

Why devops is important?

DevOps implementation is going through the roof with most of the largest software organizations around the world invested heavily in its implementation. The core values of devops is effectively based on the Agile Manifesto but with one slight change which moves the focus from creating a working software to one that is more interested in the end-to-end software service mechanism and delivery.

Why should you opt for a DevOps career?

For very long times the development and the operations teams of any software enterprise have stayed at arm’s length. But this organizational cultural shift thanks to devops a lot of changes are happening in forward-thinking enterprises. Learning devops will help you master all the skills needed in order to successfully build, operate, monitor, measure and improve the various processes in IT enterprises by better integrating development and operations. videoTitle: هل الـ DevOps engineers مطلوبين في الشركات؟
videoDescription: في الحلقة دي بنناقش كل التفاصيل المهمة عن عالم DevOps. هنتكلم عن اللي كان بيحصل في تطوير البرمجيات قبل ظهور الـ DevOps وإزاي غير المشهد تمامًا. كمان هنوضح الفرق بين DevOps وSRE، وليه بعض الشركات بتفضل تستخدم واحدة عن التانية. لو بتسأل عن فرص الشغل في المجال ده سواء في مصر أو بره، هتعرف حجم الفرص وأهميتها ومتوسط الرواتب اللي ممكن تحققها. الحلقة كمان بتجاوب على سؤال مهم: إيه اللي محتاج تعمله كخريج جديد عشان تبدأ في المجال؟ وهتديك نصايح عملية لكل اللي محتاج تكون مجهزه قبل ما تروح الإنترفيو. متفوتش الفرصة دي لو عايز تبدأ مشوارك في DevOps بخطوات ثابتة وواضحة.

videoTitle: DKT88 - DevOps Mock interview #6 (Senior/Lead DevOps engineer)
videoDescription:
Очередное мок-собеседование на канале! Сегодня в гостях Саша (не СТО) — Senior DevOps Engineer, с которым мы проходим по всему циклу разработки: от проектирования CI/CD до траблшутинга в Kubernetes. 
Много практики, лайв-кодинга и архитектурных вопросов.
Слушаем, запоминаем вопросы, влетаем за разбором на Patreon/Бусти!

🔍 ЧТО СПРАШИВАЛИ

- CI/CD: проектируем идеальный пайплайн для Java-бэкенда и спорим о порядке стейджей
- Git Internals: навигация по дереву коммитов (Detached HEAD) и правка истории
- Kubernetes: архитектура (Cluster API, Control Plane), чем на самом деле занимается Controller Manager и нюансы StatefulSet
- Troubleshooting: чиним права доступа (RBAC) в кластере и разбираем упавший Docker-билд с пропавшими пакетами
- Helm & IaC: зачем нужны Library charts, шаблонизация и структура Terraform-репозитория

⏱️ НАВИГАЦИЯ ПО ВИДЕО

00:00:00 - 🎬 Вступление и приветствие
00:01:15 - 👤 Представление гостя: Саша, Senior DevOps
00:02:37 - 📈 Карьерный путь: от SysAdmin к DevOps и K8s
🚀 ЧАСТЬ 1: CI/CD И Git
00:07:33 - Проектируем пайплайн для Java-приложения в GitLab CI
00:12:31 - Порядок стейджей: Lint, Test, SAST, Build — что важнее?
00:15:43 - Настройки репозитория и Branching Strategies
00:18:20 - Почему Feature branches лучше, чем commit в develop
00:24:30 - Задача: Git Visuals — навигация по дереву коммитов
🐳 ЧАСТЬ 2: Kubernetes
00:34:03 - Опыт с K8s: Self-hosted, Bare metal и Cluster API
00:41:00 - Сколько кластеров нужно для разработки? (Dev vs Prod)
00:42:41 - Компоненты Control Plane и роль Controller Manager
00:46:54 - Deployment vs StatefulSet: привязка PVC и реплики
00:51:58 - Topology Spread Constraints и Affinity
00:53:49 - Задача: Troubleshooting K8s (CrashLoopBackOff и RBAC)
📦 ЧАСТЬ 3: Helm & Docker
01:06:55 - Helm: Library charts, наследование чартов и Go templates
01:18:29 - Задача: Troubleshooting Docker-сборки (пропавший пакет в Ubuntu)
💻 ЧАСТЬ 4: Scription & IaC
01:35:11 - Скриптинг: парсинг OpenAPI спецификации (Bash/jq)
01:57:24 - Terraform: использование модулей и версионирование
02:04:07 - Как разделять окружения (Dev/Stage/Prod) в IaC
02:08:25 - Завершение интервью
